title: Statistics-correctness layer: typed measures, age-standard + comparability guard, CI/estimate uncertainty
context: With the pilot site's aggregate data ingested and validated (incidence-explorers-ingest-007, incidence-explorers-validate-008) and the statistical-honesty spec written (incidence-explorers-stats-spec-004), the project can implement the statistics-correctness layer (lib/stats). This layer enforces typed measures, the age-standardisation comparability guard, and CI/estimate-uncertainty handling so that dashboards and explainers cannot display misleading statistics — a core product feature, not an afterthought.
objective: Implement the statistics-correctness layer (lib/stats) as specified in incidence-explorers-stats-spec-004: typed Measure (incidence/mortality/prevalence/survival), CountOrRate, AgeStandard, and uncertainty types; a comparability guard that refuses to co-plot series with incompatible measures or age-standardisation standards on the same axis without an explicit labelled caveat; and CI/estimate-uncertainty handling that renders GLOBOCAN as modelled estimates with uncertainty, never as exact counts, and shows CIs where the source publishes them.
acceptanceCriteria: Typed Measure (incidence/mortality/prevalence/survival), CountOrRate, AgeStandard (e.g. US 2000 Standard, World Segi/Doll), and uncertainty types are implemented in lib/stats

The comparability guard refuses to co-plot two series with incompatible measures or incompatible age-standardisation standard populations on the same axis unless an explicit labelled caveat is provided; this is enforced at the library boundary and tested

GLOBOCAN DataPoints are rendered as modelled estimates with uncertainty, never as exact counts; CIs are shown where the source publishes them; short-term trend use of GLOBOCAN estimates is blocked or clearly caveated
All typed-measure and comparability-guard logic has unit tests; the statistics-correctness layer is tested independently of the dashboard layer
CI is green (build/test/lint); reviewed and signed off by the Statistics reviewer
output: A merged PR implementing the statistics-correctness layer (lib/stats) with typed measures, the age-standardisation comparability guard, and CI/estimate-uncertainty handling — the foundation for incidence-explorers-dashboard-010 (accessible dashboards) and incidence-explorers-eval-011 (eval gates).
